program_name: Fresh-bunch multi-stage scheme for high power.  1 keV, 3 kA
details:
Shift Summary

Beam Parameters:
  • Energy: 1keV (+/- 100 eV if good config exist at one of this points)  
  • Charge: nominal  
  • Current: ~3kA, we need a strong spike a the tail and uniform lasing at the tail  


Required devices:
 
  • XTCAV & OTRDMP Scr/Camera  
  • Soft X-ray Spectrometer  
  • Both dechirper  
  • Undulator BPMs 

Program
30’ General setup & tuning
120’ Establish nice single spike lasing in the tail (U17-U22)
120’ Establish slice selection - optimizing curvature / amplitude
30’ Taking data (gain length curve / spectra / orbit / gas detector / XTCAV image)

We want to measure at 350, 550, 750, 1000, 1200 and 1500 eV starting at 350 eV.
  The pulse energy is not critical, and the pulse duration have be the nominal value.

Questionnaire

Present plan to start with 525 eV SASE, 100 fs duration. Around ~11 am, will switch to two color (523 & 530 eV) fresh slice and continue this for remainder.

program_name: 7.6 GeV, First 1 Hz past STCLTS, BPM checkout
description: 7.6 GeV, First 1 Hz past STCLTS, BPM checkout
details:
1.    Setup MPS and beam rate controls to permit beam in CLTS with STCLTS, ST33, ST34 open and verify vacuum valves are open and unbypassed
2.    Initial Beam: Energy = 7.636 GeV; Rate = 1Hz;  Charge ≅ 50 pC; Power ≅ 0.4 W
3.    Load in and trim quads to 7.636 GeV configuration to design
4.    Standardize BKRCUS, BLRCUS, BRCUS1, and DL2B; set BDES to 7.636 GeV;
5.    Zero steering correctors: YCCUS8, YCUS9 BRCUS1_TRIM, YCBP33, YCBP34 and XCCUS10, XCBP33, XCBP34 [plus LTUS correctors]
6.    With STCLTS IN, put beam onto STCLTS using “minimal” tweak on BKRCUS, typically from 7.636 to about 8.0 or so (in the range of 5%-9% more than BLRCUS value
7.    Turn on CLTS energy feedback
8.    Extract STCLTS and send beam past Muon Wall (controlled tweak fest with steering correctors; use BRCUS1 trim as a “last resort.”) In tandem:
9.    Look for BPM signals on BPMCUS8 thru BPMBP34; time BPMs as needed
10.    MPS, BPM, Timing Controls adjustments and calibrations as necessary
11.    Commission or otherwise calibrate BPMs downstream of BPMCUS8 and into LTUS, to either TDKIKS or TDUNDS.
12.    MPS, BPM, Timing Controls adjustments and calibrations as necessary
13.    Nominal Beam: Energy = 7.636 GeV; Rate = 30 Hz;  Charge <125 pC; Power <29 W
14.    Check BKRCUS timing for maximum kick TDES
15.    Measure betatron aperture by scanning BKRCUS over range till TMIT drops on either end of strength sweep; note extreme values; set back to 7.636GeV; record a reference orbit
16.    Measure energy aperture by sweeping energy feedback setpoint; note extreme values; set back to 7.636 GeV; record a reference orbit
17.    Repeat June 20, 2020 oscillation data using as many downstream BPMs as available
18.    Look for BKRCUS field leakage onto CLTH beam using hardline BSA; look for cross talk for BKRCUS timed for extraction and at standby
19.    Measure kicker jitter in nominal running configuration using BSA.
poc: T. Smith, Sheppard, Bohler, Hoobler, Bianchini

details:
Part 3 (gated to 10 Hz rate until we pull TDUNDB):

1.     2 Hours – 25 W (250 pC, 10 GeV, 10 Hz):For Survey: To TDKIKB, at least 25 W. Beam on TDKIKB for 2 hours to survey outside areas. (No residual survey. Reserved for Part 4 HP test.)

2.     2 Hours – 25 W (250 pC, 10 GeV, 10 Hz):For Survey: Then beam for 2 hours on TDUNDB to survey the BTH and outside areas.

3.     1 Hour – Residual radiation measurement of TDUNDB (1 hour BTH access) immediately at the end of the shift.

(gated to 10 Hz rate until we pull TDUNDB):


1.     1 Hour – 5 W (125 pC, 4 GeV, 10 Hz): LION SXR to TDUNDB (CD-SSBCS-07-12-19-01)

2.     1 Hour – 25 W (250 pC, 10 GeV, 10 Hz): PIC Beam Checkout BSY to TDUNDB (CD-SS-BCS-07-12-15-01)

PART 4 (3 hours)

- high power beam (hundred of watts) on TDKIK1S

- prompt radiation survey around HH and B911

- residual radiation survey around TDKIK1S

program_name: TMO Comm., 4.4 GeV, 300 eV to start

details:
https://pswww.slac.stanford.edu/questionnaire_slac/proposal_questionnaire/run18/tmoc00118

A large range of photon energies from 300-2000 eV are needed for commissioning. Doing all of this with only the starting K is likely aggressive as performance may drop dramatically below K=3.

Strategy is to start at max K for lowest photon energy. Then lower K to get the higher energies, to a point.


Suggest using 4.4 GeV for 300-800 eV, then ramp up to 7.15 GeV to do 800-2000 eV. Increase K using the SXU photon energy calculator in ULT for steps at a given beam energy.



Some taper tuning may be required for different starting K if performance droops (later start for post saturation taper, for example).

https://pswww.slac.stanford.edu/questionnaire_slac/proposal_questionnaire/run18/tmoc00118

Strategy is to start at max K for lowest photon energy. Then lower K to get the higher energies, to a point.


Suggest using 4.4 GeV for 300-800 eV, then ramp up to 7.15 GeV to do 800-2000 eV. Increase K using the SXU photon energy calculator in ULT for steps at a given beam energy.



Some taper tuning may be required for different starting K if performance droops (later start for post saturation taper, for example).

details:
Start at 700eV (will move to 300 eV toward middle of shift, and may go higher in energy later in shift) 
Longer pulses 150-200 fs duration 
Lots of sharp SASE spikes are desired

chemRIXS Comm (Dakovski) (start at 700eV change per notes)

Energy at 700 eV. We would like to request temporally long pulses, but we don’t require much energy (>100 uJ but ideally <1 mJ).

We expect to spend most of the shift at 700 eV. In the less likely event we change energy the lowest energy will be 400 eV and the highest will be 1000 eV.

commissioning tasks:

ATM: (400 eV)

  • X-ray laser spatial overlap @ ATM-YAG
  • Coarse timing with MDM (diode)
  • Setup 10 Hz to BSY (dark shots for background)
  • Start looking for signal with OPAL.
  • Find signal on tmo_fzppiranah.  
  • Record data on timing-jitter for 30-60 minutes

MBES: (530 eV)
  • Continue focus scanning with MBES

commissioning tasks:

ATM: (400 eV)

  • X-ray laser spatial overlap @ ATM-YAG
  • Coarse timing with MSM (diode)
  • Setup 10 Hz to BSY (dark shots for background)
  • Start looking for signal with OPAL.
  • Find signal on tmo_fzppiranah.  
  • Record data on timing-jitter for 30-60 minutes

MBES: (400 eV)
  • Magnet Alignment Scans: No ion extraction, no retardation
    • Record 0V retardation data (Argon)
  • Magnet Alignment with Retardation: (-150 V retardation)
    • Record data in Argon
  • Apply Ion Extraction, repeat Z-scan
  • Scan chamber to find focus position. 
  • Repeat measurements with N2.

commissioning tasks:

IM1K4 (Phil)

  • Change position of scintillation crystal. 

MBES: (400 eV -> 530 eV)
  • Check FEX
  • Magnet Alignment with Retardation:
    • (-100, 150, 200 V retardation)
    • Record data in Argon
  • Apply Ion Extraction (-178 V)
    • (-100, 150, 200, 250, 300 V)
  • Move to focus location
    • Scan Mag-Z to find focus with ions
    • Move SQ1-Z to the focus location
    • With no extraction field scan SQ-1 Y to optimize electrons
    • Record electron/ion spectrum in Ar
  • Optimize focus with nonlinear electron signal
    • Switch to N2 @ 530 eV
    • 300 V retardation, Magnet off
    • Move SQ-1 in Z recording data (-2:0.5:2 mm)
    • compensate for Y 
  • Record ion/electron coincidence data at the focus.

• Beam Alignment (400 eV)

    • Check beam position on all imagers to IM5K4

    • Check beam focus at IP1 using WFS

    • Power meters? (check average power of FEL and decide if PM makes sense)

  • ATM work

    • Mark beam position on IMATM camera

    • Mark beam position on scope

    • QADC triggering - ?

  • FZP work

    • Align spectrometer


  • MBES Alignment: (Argon Target, 400 eV)

    • Rough alignment of chamber (Flight tube) based on beam.

    • Test solenoid coil field direction

    • Optimize MBES magnet position based on electron counts

    • Optimize gas needle position

    • Test secondary coil (directionality)

  • Test extraction/retardation scheme to use Mini-ToF (300 eV across IP)

  • LAMP scan-y - sensitivity of electron signal to LAMP vertical motion

  • LAMP scan-z to find x-ray focus for electrons

• x-ray and laser alignment

  • ATM signal optimization, and time-axis calibration

  • configuration testing

    • change configuration

    • relocate rough and fine timing

    • data collection

• Beam Alignment (400 eV)

    • Check beam position on all imagers to IM5K4

    • Check beam focus at IP1 using WFS

    • Run KB1 Power Meter script from Basil

  • FZP work

    • Align spectrometer

    • Measure (and record) spectra at 100 H and 500 H repetition rates to compare pulse energies. 

  • MBES (Argon Target, 400 eV)

    • LAMP scan-y - sensitivity of electron signal to LAMP vertical motion 
      Repeat this scan from last shift (+2mm/-4mm)

    • LAMP scan-z to find x-ray focus for electrons (+- 1 cm, 2mm steps)

    • Test secondary coil (directionality) - record data for both orientations.

program_name: injector and linac Recovery and FEL at 930eV
description: injector and linac Recovery and FEL at 930eV
details:
* Recover the beam after FACET access.
* save a score
* verify the injector emittance, (and bunch length if STCAV is ready)
* optimize the injector emittance. Check downstream WS emittance if possible.
* LEM up to 3.7 GeV, check 400eV performance. Save a score.
* change to FEL at 930eV,  tuning at 75pC. 
   * check HL setting effect
   * check the HL area orbit effect
* check 930eV FEL vs higher charge or lower charge.
* set up Badger to optimize 930eV with quads at 75 or other preferred charge.

details:
injector, optimization and characterization:

* 65pC.  iris=1.0mm
* emittance / STCAV optimization (for reference: buncher -25deg, CAV1 =-10deg , 75pC HTR BLEN =2000; 90pC, HTR BLEN =2600)
 * ImpactT bunchmark:  1) VCCB image, 2)HTR BLEN vs SOL1,  3) OTR0H04 rms size vs SOL1 or SOL2, 4) buncher phase vs HTR BLEN
 * COL0 and 0H04. 

* FEL optimization with downstream matching quads (COL1, EMIT2, DOG, LTUS): Badger, Ocelot; CQ and LTUS dispersion quads.
